symptoms.symptom_name: Intractable nausea with anorexia and near-cessation of oral intake

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Uraemic toxin accumulation is a classic cause of anorexia and nausea; when it stops intake it also drives superimposed AKI.

symptoms.symptom_name: Generalised pruritus without a primary rash

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Uraemic pruritus is a whole-body itch without a primary rash — a hallmark of advanced renal dysfunction.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Primary dermatological cause of itch (e.g. scabies)
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of rash, burrows or web-space involvement argues against a primary skin disease.

symptoms.symptom_name: Progressive bilateral peripheral pitting oedema (ankles to knees)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Salt and water retention from failing kidneys produces dependent oedema.
Bilateral dependent oedema is also cardiac; the two cannot be separated without exam and bloods.

symptoms.symptom_name: Profound progressive fatigue with functional decline

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Uraemia and anaemia of CKD both cause fatigue; functional decline in a frail elder is a red-flag signal of acute deterioration.
Early loss of urinary concentrating ability presents as nocturia in chronic kidney disease.
Electrolyte and neuromuscular disturbance of renal failure causes cramps; also a soft pointer to electrolyte derangement risk.

symptoms.symptom_name: Denies vomiting and denies shortness of breath

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of breathlessness lowers (does not exclude) fluid overload of cardiac origin and current pulmonary oedema.
No vomiting or diarrhoea, and a chronic progressive course, argues against acute gastroenteritis.

symptoms.symptom_name: Elevated blood pressure 165/95 mmHg (facility reading) despite regular antihypertensives

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Fluid retention and renin-angiotensin activation cause treatment-refractory hypertension; patient/caregiver-reported so admissible in telehealth, unverified.
Reduced erythropoietin causes normocytic normochromic anaemia; pallor may be visible on video but confirmation and degree require FBC — sealed clinician/lab finding.
Uraemic and electrolyte-related neuromuscular disturbance; requires hands-on neurological examination — not obtainable via telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Reduced eGFR (below 60 mL/min/1.73m2 for over three months)

investigation_details.expected_result: eGFR below 60 mL/min/1.73m2, in keeping with chronic kidney disease with acute deterioration
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: CKD stage and acute deterioration remain unquantified; severity and dialysis-threshold decisions cannot be made.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: eGFR is the defining measure of renal function; sealed clinician-ordered investigation, never available in telehealth.

investigation_details.expected_result: Possible hyperkalaemia and other derangement in advancing renal failure with poor intake

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Electrolyte derangement, especially hyperkalaemia, is the immediately dangerous complication that bloods must exclude; not available in telehealth.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Nausea/anorexia, rash-free generalised pruritus, progressive oedema, profound fatigue and refractory hypertension together point strongly to advancing renal dysfunction with uraemia in a frail elder.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Urea, electrolytes and creatinine with eGFR
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Defines renal function, quantifies acute-on-chronic deterioration and detects dangerous hyperkalaemia; the AI Doctor should recognise this is required and arrange in-person urgent bloods, not attempt to obtain or interpret them remotely.
Assesses the normocytic anaemia of CKD contributing to fatigue and pallor.
Assesses kidney size, obstruction and chronicity; arranged by the in-person clinician, not orderable via telehealth.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Skin scraping / dermatological work-up for scabies
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: The rash-free generalised itch with systemic decline is not a primary skin disease; over-investigating the skin would anchor on the wrong system.
